elements.sent-2411-001: We investigate the reflected entropy for bipartite mixed state configurations in a $T\bar{T}$ deformed boundary conformal field theory in $2$ dimensions (BCFT$_2$).
elements.sent-2411-002: The bulk dual is described by asymptotically AdS$_3$ geometries with the cut off surface pushed deeper into the bulk and truncated by an end of the world brane.
elements.sent-2411-003: We obtain the reflected entropy up to a linear order in the radial cut-off for static and time dependent configurations involving an eternal black hole, from the island and defect extremal surface (DES) prescriptions in the context of the deformed AdS/BCFT.
elements.sent-2411-004: We observe agreement of the leading order correction for all cases between the two prescriptions.
elements.sent-2411-005: We also obtain the analogous of the Page curves for the reflected entropy and investigate the modification due to the $T\bar{T}$ deformation.
elements.sent-2411-006: The black hole information loss problem has been a central topic of research for several decades, spurring significant progress in semiclassical and quantum gravity.
elements.sent-2411-007: Recent studies have made impactful strides towards resolving this problem through the island framework that provide access to regions inside black hole geometries at late times \cite{Almheiri:2019hni, Almheiri:2019psf, Almheiri:2019qdq, Almheiri:2019psy, Almheiri:2019yqk, Almheiri:2020cfm .}
elements.sent-2411-008: Through this framework, the Page Curve \cite{Page:1993wv, Page:1993df, Page:2013dx for the von Neumann entropy of the Hawking radiation was reproduced for a CFT coupled to a theory of gravity by including the ``island'' inside the gravitational region in the entanglement wedge of the radiation bath.}
elements.sent-2411-009: The emergence of these islands, is attributed to the late time dominance of replica wormhole saddles in the gravitational path integral for the R\'enyi entanglement entropy.
elements.sent-2411-010: The so called island formula was inspired by the quantum extremal surface (QES) prescription \cite{Engelhardt:2014gca which describes the quantum corrections to the holographic entanglement entropy \cite{Ryu:2006bv, Hubeny:2007xt, Faulkner:2013ana .}}
elements.sent-2411-011: A more elegant and insightful interpretation of the island formula may be provided by the double holography framework \cite{Almheiri:2019hni, Sully:2020pza, Rozali:2019day, Chen:2020uac, Chen:2020hmv, Grimaldi:2022suv, Suzuki:2022xwv, Geng:2020qvw, Geng:2020fxl, Geng:2021iyq, Geng:2021mic, Geng:2021hlu .}
elements.sent-2411-012: This involves considering the CFT$_d$ coupled to the semiclassical gravity to be holographic and be described by a $(d+1)$-dimensional gravity theory.
elements.sent-2411-013: In the literature, this higher dimensional picture have been termed as the bulk description for the original setup which is termed as the brane description.
elements.sent-2411-014: This gravity-plus-matter system in the brane description may, in turn be described through a quantum mechanical system residing on the boundary of the CFT$_d$ which was dubbed as the boundary description.
elements.sent-2411-015: In this double holographic setup, the computation of the entanglement entropy through the island formula in the brane description translates to its holographic characterization through the Ryu-Takayanagi (RT) prescription in the bulk AdS$_{d+1}$ geometry.
elements.sent-2411-016: Diverging from the above, boundary conformal field theories (BCFTs) \cite{Cardy:2004hm, Takayanagi:2011zk, Fujita:2011fp, Sully:2020pza, Kastikainen:2021ybu , defined as CFTs on a manifold with a boundary, have garnered significant interest in recent years.}
elements.sent-2411-017: The holographic dual to a BCFT$_2$ is described by an asymptotically AdS$_3$ spacetime bounded by an end-of-the-world (EOW) brane with Neumann boundary conditions.
elements.sent-2411-018: The holographic entanglement entropy formula \cite{Takayanagi:2011zk, Fujita:2011fp for this framework involves modification of the homology condition to incorporate extremal surfaces anchored to the EOW brane as well.}
elements.sent-2411-019: In \cite{Deng:2020ent , the authors explore an extension of this AdS$_3$/BCFT$_2$ duality, where the Neumann boundary condition on the EOW brane is modified by addition of defect conformal matter.}
elements.sent-2411-020: The works \cite{Deng:2020ent, Chu:2021gdb introduce the defect extremal surface (DES) formula which extends the RT prescription by incorporating contributions from defect matter fields in the context of defect AdS$_3$/BCFT$_2$.}
elements.sent-2411-021: Notably, the DES formula in this defect AdS$_3$/BCFT$_2$ has been proposed and verified as the doubly holographic counterpart of the island formula in the lower dimensional effective description.\footnote{Note that in \cite{Deng:2020ent, Chu:2021gdb, Li:2021dmf, Basu:2022reu, Shao:2022wrm , the brane perspective described in the double holographic framework has been termed as the lower dimensional effective description as the authors obtained the brane perspective through partial dimensional reduction of the bulk AdS$_3$ geometry with the EOW brane.}}
elements.sent-2411-022: We will use these two terminologies interchangeably in this article.
elements.sent-2411-023: While entanglement entropy is a suitable metric for investigating the entanglement structure of bipartite pure states, it fails to accurately characterize the mixed state entanglement structure.
elements.sent-2411-024: For systems involving bipartite mixed states, it is preferable to utilize alternative (computable) measures such as reflected entropy \cite{Dutta:2019gen, Akers:2021pvd , entanglement negativity \cite{Vidal:2002zz, Plenio:2005cwa , entanglement of purification \cite{Takayanagi:2017knl, Nguyen:2017yqw , and balanced partial entanglement entropy \cite{Wen:2021qgx .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-025: These measures offer a more nuanced characterization of the complex entanglement structure in mixed states.
elements.sent-2411-026: Recognizing that the combined state of a black hole and its radiation may not always be pure, it is important to generalize the island framework for mixed states.
elements.sent-2411-027: The authors in \cite{Li:2020ceg, Chandrasekaran:2020qtn provided this generalization in terms of the reflected entropy \cite{Dutta:2019gen, Jeong:2019xdr which was introduced to investigate bipartite mixed states.}}
elements.sent-2411-028: It is based on canonical purification of the given mixed state and is described holographically in terms of the area of the bulk entanglement wedge cross-section (EWCS) \cite{Takayanagi:2017knl, Nguyen:2017yqw .}
elements.sent-2411-029: Subsequently, in \cite{Li:2021dmf , a DES formula for the reflected entropy was forwarded which was shown to be the bulk counterpart of the island formula for the reflected entropy in the defect AdS$_3$/BCFT$_2$ scenario.\footnote{A similar DES formula for entanglement negativity was also forwarded in \cite{Basu:2022reu, Shao:2022wrm which provides double holographic description for the island formula for the entanglement negativity \cite{KumarBasak:2020ams .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-030: This equivalence provides a robust framework for studying the entanglement in mixed states within these holographic settings.
elements.sent-2411-031: On a different note, an interesting behaviour of a CFT$_2$ deformed by an irrelevant operator composed of the stress-energy tensor was observed in \cite{Zamolodchikov:2004ce .}
elements.sent-2411-032: Such $T \bar{T}$-deformed theories retained their integrability and are solvable as the energy spectrum, partition function and S-matrix are exactly determinable \cite{Zamolodchikov:2004ce, Cavaglia:2016oda, Smirnov:2016lqw .}
elements.sent-2411-033: The holographic dual for such deformed theories was forwarded in \cite{McGough:2016lol where the asymptotic boundary of bulk AdS$_3$ geometry is now at a finite radial distance satisfying Dirichlet boundary conditions.\footnote{Note that alternative proposals for the holographic duality of these $T \bar{T}$ deformed theories have also been proposed in the literature, such as \cite{Hirano:2020nwq, Kraus:2018xrn, Guica:2019nzm .}}}
elements.sent-2411-034: It is expected that they will coincide under certain limits, however the exact matching for general cases remains a non-trivial open issue.
elements.sent-2411-035: This proposal has been further substantiated by subsequent developments \cite{Shyam:2017znq, Kraus:2018xrn, Cottrell:2018skz, Taylor:2018xcy, Hartman:2018tkw, Shyam:2018sro, Caputa:2019pam, Giveon:2017myj, Asrat:2017tzd, Donnelly:2018bef, Lewkowycz:2019xse, Chen:2018eqk, Banerjee:2019ewu, Jeong:2019ylz, Murdia:2019fax, Park:2018snf, Asrat:2019end, He:2019vzf, Grieninger:2019zts, Khoeini-Moghaddam:2020ymm, Basu:2024bal, Basu:2023aqz, Basu:2023bov, Basu:2024enr .}
elements.sent-2411-036: These studies have solidified the understanding and applications of $T\bar{T}$ deformations in holographic contexts.
elements.sent-2411-037: The two kind of boundaries discussed earlier, the EOW brane in the context of AdS/BCFT, and the asymptotic boundary at the finite cut-off surfaces in holographic dual of $T \bar{T}$ deformed theories, have very different nature because of distinct boundary conditions governing them.
elements.sent-2411-038: In order to elucidate these differences, the authors in \cite{Deng:2023pjs,Deng:2024dct investigated a spacetime with both of these boundaries.}
elements.sent-2411-039: In order to have a transparent boundary between the two surfaces, they introduced the same matter field on the EOW as is present at the asymptotic boundary in the dual picture.
elements.sent-2411-040: In the boundary description, this corresponds to a $T \bar T$ deformation of a BCFT$_2$, which may be obtained through a partial dimensional reduction of a braneworld gravity theory glued to a non-conformal bath with $T\bar{T}$ deformation \cite{Deng:2023pjs .}
elements.sent-2411-041: The authors investigated the entanglement structure of bipartite pure states in such a model through the entanglement entropy.
elements.sent-2411-042: The above advancements underscore the importance of characterizing mixed state entanglement in AdS geometries that incorporate both an EOW brane and a finite radial cut-off surface.
elements.sent-2411-043: To this end, we utilize the reflected entropy to study bipartite mixed states in asymptotically AdS$_3$ geometries with a finite radial cut-off, which originates from $T\bar{T}$ deformation, and is truncated by an EOW brane.
elements.sent-2411-044: We utilize the DES formula for the reflected entropy to compute bulk reflected entropy through the EWCS and compare these with island computation in the lower dimensional effective description.
elements.sent-2411-045: Additionally, we extend our analysis to time-dependent configurations, involving black hole and radiation subsystems in an effective lower-dimensional theory.
elements.sent-2411-046: The remainder of this article is structured as follows.
elements.sent-2411-047: In \cref{sec:review , we briefly review various aspects of $T\bar{T}$ deformation, reflected entropy, and the island and DES formulas that are essential for computing reflected entropy.}
elements.sent-2411-048: In \cref{sec:Zero_Temp , we introduce the DES formula for calculating reflected entropy in asymptotically AdS$_3$ geometries with a finite radial cut-off bounded by an EOW brane.}
elements.sent-2411-049: We compute the reflected entropy for both adjacent and disjoint intervals, considering various possible entanglement entropy phases in a static time slice, and demonstrate the equivalence of the DES and the island formula.
elements.sent-2411-050: In \cref{sec:Finite_Temp , we extend our analysis to time-dependent configurations.}
elements.sent-2411-051: We start by reviewing the essentials of the eternal black hole in the two-dimensional effective picture, followed by describing the DES and island computations for the reflected entropy between interior regions of the black hole, between the black hole and radiation in the bath system, and between radiation subsystems.
elements.sent-2411-052: Finally, we summarize our findings in \cref{sec:conc .}

elements.section-2411-004: Time dependent reflected entropy in black hole evaporation
elements.sent-2411-208: In this section we aim to study the reflected entropy due to $T\bar{T}$ deformation in a time dependent AdS$_3$/BCFT$_2$ setting.
elements.sent-2411-209: It is observed that in this setting an eternal black hole emerges from the boundary description.
elements.sent-2411-210: We show that in a manner similar to the previous section, there is an agreement in both the boundary and bulk theories.
elements.sent-2411-211: In this section we compute the reflected entropy taking into account $T\bar{T}$ deformation between black hole and radiation, for two parts of black hole interior, and between left and right radiation.
elements.sent-2411-212: We also find out the correction in the Page time due to $T\bar{T}$ deformation.
elements.subsection-2411-011: The emergence of a 2d eternal black hole
elements.sent-2411-213: We begin by reviewing how a 2d eternal black hole emerges from the AdS$_3$/BCFT$_2$ model \cite{Li:2021dmf,Rozali:2019day,Chu:2021gdb .}
elements.sent-2411-214: As discussed in the previous section, the holographic dual to a 2d BCFT is an AdS$_3$ geometry with a codimension-1 EOW brane.
elements.sent-2411-215: The metric of Euclidean AdS$_3$ bulk is given as \begin{equation}ds^2 =\frac{\ell^2}{z^2} (d\tau^2+dz^2+dx^2) \notag = d\sigma^2+\frac{\ell^2 \cosh^2 \frac{\sigma}{\ell}}{y^2}\left( d\tau^2+dy^2 \right).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-216: We consider the bulk geometry to be bounded by a BCFT defined on a half plane given by $ (x,\tau >0)$\footnote{In Euclidean spacetime, spacelike and timelike coordinates are equivalent, and thus $x$ and $\tau$ can be interchanged as per convenience.. .}
elements.sent-2411-217: The EOW brane is located at $\tau=-z\sinh \frac{\sigma_0 {\ell}$, with $\sigma_0$ being a constant.}
elements.figure-2411-013: \includegraphics[scale=0.8]{GCT} \caption{In the first step a global conformal transformation takes us from from Euclidean AdS$_3$/BCFT$_2$ to a thermofield double state. It is followed by analytically continuing $\tau ' \to it'$ which provides us the Lorentz view of the TFD.} \label{fig_GCT}
elements.sent-2411-218: Using the set of global conformal transformations as follows \begin{equation}\label{eq_gct} \tau = \frac{2(x'^2+\tau '^2+z'^2-1)}{(\tau '+1)^2+x'^2+z'^2}, \notag x = \frac{4x'}{(\tau '+1)^2+x'^2+z'^2}, z = \frac{4z'}{(\tau '+1)^2+x'^2+z'^2}, \notag\end{equation} the boundary of the BCFT and the EOW brane may be mapped to a circle and a part of a sphere respectively as \begin{equation}\label{eq_2dbh} x'^2+\tau '^2 =1, \ \ \ \ \ \ (z'+\sinh \frac{\sigma_0}{\ell})^2 + x'^2 +\tau '^2 = \cosh ^2 \frac{\sigma_0}{\ell}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-219: Note that the global conformal transformations given by \cref{eq_gct ensure that the metric of the bulk as well as the metric induced on the EOW brane is preserved.}
elements.sent-2411-220: The inverse transformations to \cref{eq_gct are provided in \cite{Basu:2022reu .}}
elements.sent-2411-221: From the above bulk configuration, a two-sided 2d eternal black hole may be obtained using the partial Randall-Sundrum reduction and AdS$_3$/BCFT$_2$ correspondence \cite{Deng:2020ent .}
elements.sent-2411-222: The black hole appears on the EOW brane, which in the effective 2d description is coupled to the BCFT$_2$ outside the circle in \cref{eq_2dbh .}
elements.sent-2411-223: This hybrid manifold may be described as analytical continuation of $\tau ' \to it'$ followed by introduction of the Rindler coordinates $(X,T)$ as \cite{Almheiri:2019qdq \begin{equation}\label{eq_rt} x' = e^X \cosh T, \ \ \ \ \ \ t'= e^X \sinh T,\end{equation} which capture the near-horizon geometry of the black hole.}
elements.formula-2411-077: \begin{equation}\label{eq_rt} x' = e^X \cosh T, \ \ \ \ \ \ t'= e^X \sinh T,\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-224: As a result, the EOW brane is shifted to $\tau=-(z-z_c)\sinh \frac{\sigma_0 {\ell}$.}
elements.sent-2411-225: The boundary of the BCFT and the brane profile in \cref{eq_2dbh is thus modified as \begin{equation}x'^2+\tau '^2 =1-z_c'^2, \ \ \ \ \ \ (z'+\sinh \frac{\sigma_0}{\ell})^2 + x'^2 +\tau '^2 = \cosh ^2 \frac{\sigma_0}{\ell}+2z_c'\sinh \frac{\sigma_0}{\ell},\end{equation} respectively, where the relation between $z_c'$ and $z_c$ is provided by \cref{eq_gct .}}
elements.sent-2411-226: Subsequently, we may once again obtain the two-sided 2d eternal black hole via transformations in \cref{eq_rt , using the partial Randall-Sundrum reduction and AdS$_3$/BCFT$_2$ correspondence.}
elements.sent-2411-227: The UV cut-off in the Rindler coordinates $z_R$ can be related to $z_c'$ as \begin{equation}\label{eq_rind_cutoff} z_c'=z_Re^X,\end{equation} where $z_R$ is assumed to be a constant everywhere.
elements.subsection-2411-012: Reflected entropy between black hole interiors
elements.sent-2411-228: In this section we compute the time dependent reflected entropy between different regions of the black hole interior in presence of $T\bar{T}$ deformation.
elements.sent-2411-229: As illustrated in \cref{fig_BBC,fig_BBDC , the black hole region is defined by the spacelike interval between points $Q \equiv (\tau_1',-x_1',z_1')$ and $P \equiv (\tau_1',x_1',z_1')$ \cite{Li:2021dmf,Chu:2021gdb .}}

elements.sent-2411-362: In this article, we investigate the structure of mixed state entanglement through the framework of reflected entropy in a $T\bar{T}$-deformed theory with a boundary.
elements.sent-2411-363: Our focus is on an AdS$_3$ model characterized by a finite radial cut-off, induced by the $T\bar{T}$ deformation, and truncated by an end-of-the-world (EOW) brane containing defect matter.
elements.sent-2411-364: As discussed in \cite{Deng:2023pjs , such a model can naturally arise from a partial reduction, specifically the Karch-Randall reduction, which applies to a segment of the AdS bulk between a finite-tension and a zero-tension brane.}
elements.sent-2411-365: The resulting brane-world gravity is governed by Neumann boundary conditions on the EOW brane, while we apply AdS/CFT duality for Dirichlet boundary conditions on the finite cut-off surface.
elements.sent-2411-366: In this context, we utilize the defect extremal surface (DES) formula—essentially a double-holographic counterpart to the island formula—for calculating the reflected entropy of bipartite mixed states within the defect AdS/BCFT correspondence.
elements.sent-2411-367: This DES formula offers a novel approach to understanding entanglement in such deformed setups, bridging insights from both holographic duality and boundary theories.
elements.sent-2411-368: We begin by computing the reflected entropy for both adjacent and disjoint intervals in static scenarios, considering various possible entanglement entropy phases.
elements.sent-2411-369: Our findings demonstrate that the results from the DES formula are equivalent to those obtained via the island formula, up to linear orders in the radial cut-off $z_c$, thereby reinforcing the validity of our approach.
elements.sent-2411-370: We then extend our analysis to time dependent configurations involving an eternal black hole coupled to a radiation bath within an effective lower-dimensional model.
elements.sent-2411-371: In this context, we compute the reflected entropy between black hole interior subsystems, between black hole and radiation subsystems, and between radiation subsystems using both the island and DES formulas.
elements.sent-2411-372: We also examine the time evolution of reflected entropy for these configurations and plot the analogous of the Page curves for the reflected entropy, considering scenarios with and without $T\bar{T}$ deformation.
elements.sent-2411-373: Once again, we find that the results from boundary and bulk calculations are consistent up to the first order in the radial cut-off for time-dependent cases, providing a strong consistency check for the proposal discussed earlier.
elements.sent-2411-374: There are several future directions to explore.
elements.sent-2411-375: One potential avenue is to investigate the correction to reflected entropy at all orders in the radial cutoff for $T \bar{T}$ deformed BCFT and to verify the equivalence between the island and the DES formula in this scenarios.
elements.sent-2411-376: Another intriguing prospect is to explore the effects of a non-constant brane tension on the results presented here.
elements.sent-2411-377: Furthermore it would be interesting to investigate other mixed state entanglement and correlation measures such as entanglement negativity, odd entanglement entropy, balance partial entanglement and multipartite entanglement in the context of such $T\bar{T}$ deformed BCFT.
elements.sent-2411-378: We leave these interesting open issue for future consideration.. .

elements.sent-2411-387: The black hole information loss problem has been a central topic of research for several decades, spurring significant progress in semi-classical and quantum gravity.
elements.sent-2411-388: The so called island formula was inspired by the quantum extremal surface (QES) prescription \cite{Engelhardt:2014gca which describes the quantum corrections to the holographic entanglement entropy \cite{Ryu:2006bv, Hubeny:2007xt, Faulkner:2013ana,Geng:2024xpj .}}
elements.sent-2411-389: A more elegant and insightful interpretation of the island formula may be provided by the double holography framework \cite{Almheiri:2019hni, Sully:2020pza, Rozali:2019day, Chen:2020uac, Chen:2020hmv, Grimaldi:2022suv, Suzuki:2022xwv, Geng:2020qvw, Geng:2020fxl, Geng:2021iyq, Geng:2021mic, Geng:2021hlu,Geng:2024xpj,Karch:2022rvr .}
elements.sent-2411-390: In \cite{Deng:2020ent , the authors explore an extension of this AdS$_3$/BCFT$_2$ duality, where the Neumann boundary condition on the EOW brane is modified by addition of defect conformal matter.\footnote{The term ``defect matter'' refers to degrees of freedom that are localized on a defect, a lower dimensional submanifold, within the full spacetime.}}
elements.sent-2411-391: While the original island framework was primarily developed for pure states, many physically relevant situations—such as subsystems within the Hawking radiation or black holes coupled to non-isolated environments—often lead to mixed states.
elements.sent-2411-392: As stated above, in such scenarios, it becomes imperative to generalize the island framework to accommodate mixed states, enabling a more detailed description of entanglement structures in gravitational settings.
elements.sent-2411-393: The authors in \cite{Li:2020ceg, Chandrasekaran:2020qtn provided this generalization in terms of the reflected entropy \cite{Dutta:2019gen, Jeong:2019xdr .}}
elements.sent-2411-394: Reflected entropy is based on canonical purification of the given mixed state and is described holographically in terms of the area of the bulk entanglement wedge cross-section (EWCS) \cite{Takayanagi:2017knl, Nguyen:2017yqw .}
elements.sent-2411-395: Subsequently, in \cite{Li:2021dmf , a DES formula for the reflected entropy was forwarded which was shown to be the bulk counterpart of the island formula for the reflected entropy in the defect AdS$_3$/BCFT$_2$ scenario.\footnote{Similar DES formulas for entanglement negativity were also forwarded in \cite{Basu:2022reu, Shao:2022wrm which provide double holographic description for the island formula for the entanglement negativity \cite{KumarBasak:2020ams .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-396: Motivated by the desire to test the robustness of the island framework for the reflected entropy and its doubly holographic generalizations, it is imperative to investigate them in less conventional gravitational settings.
elements.sent-2411-397: In this context, CFTs deformed by an irrelevant operator composed of the stress-energy tensor \cite{Zamolodchikov:2004ce, Cavaglia:2016oda, Smirnov:2016lqw provide a fertile testing ground due to their solvable nature.}
elements.sent-2411-398: These irrelevant deformations introduce a finite cutoff in the dual AdS$_3$ geometries \cite{McGough:2016lol satisfying Dirichlet boundary conditions,\footnote{Note that alternative proposals for the holographic duality of these $T \bar{T}$ deformed theories have also been proposed in the literature, such as \cite{Hirano:2020nwq, Kraus:2018xrn, Guica:2019nzm,Dubovsky:2018bmo, Tolley:2019nmm .}}}
elements.sent-2411-399: We here consider such deformations of BCFTs which provide a natural toy model to investigate the island formalism.
elements.sent-2411-400: The dual AdS geometry with an EOW brane now also involves a finite cut-off, which adds subtlety to the standard AdS/BCFT correspondence and demands a careful analysis.
elements.sent-2411-401: While the island formula and its reflected entropy analogue have been primarily studied in standard asymptotically AdS spacetimes, it is not a priori clear whether their structure survives in such geometries with finite radial cutoff and modified boundary conditions.
elements.sent-2411-402: Investigating mixed state entanglement in such setups, particularly through computable measures like reflected entropy, therefore provides a test and extends the applicability of the island paradigm in a more intricate setting.
elements.sent-2411-403: The two kind of boundaries discussed earlier, namely the EOW brane in the context of AdS/BCFT, and the asymptotic boundary at the finite cut-off surfaces in holographic dual of $T \bar{T}$ deformed theories, have very different nature because of distinct boundary conditions governing them.
elements.sent-2411-404: In this subsection, we briefly review the AdS$_3$/BCFT$_2$ correspondence, proposed in \cite{Takayanagi:2011zk,Fujita:2011fp .}
elements.sent-2411-405: A boundary conformal field theory (BCFT) is a conformal field theory (CFT) defined on a manifold $\mathcal{M}$ with boundary $\partial\mathcal{M}$, where conformal boundary conditions are specified.
elements.sent-2411-406: It was shown in \cite{Cardy:2004hm that the holographic dual of a BCFT is given by an asymptotically AdS spacetime $\mathcal{N}$ truncated by an end-of-world (EOW) brane $\mathcal{Q}$ on which the Neumann boundary condition is imposed.}
elements.sent-2411-407: The bulk action is given by \cite{Takayanagi:2011zk,Fujita:2011fp \begin{equation}\label{AdS/BCFT-action} I_0=-\frac{1}{16\pi G_N}\int_N\sqrt{g}(R-2\Lambda)-\frac{1}{8\pi G_N}\int_Q\sqrt{h}(K-(d-1)T)\,.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-408: Here $K$ represents the trace of the extrinsic curvature on the EOW brane with tension $T$.
elements.sent-2411-409: By varying the above action with respect to the induced metric on the brane $h_{ab}$, we can determine the brane trajectory, which consequently leads to the Neumann boundary conditions \begin{equation}K_{ab}=(K-T)h_{ab}\,.\label{NBC}\end{equation} The AdS$_3$/BCFT$_2$ framework was further extended in \cite{Deng:2020ent,Chu:2021gdb by incorporating conformal matter (essentially a two-dimensional CFT) on a tensionless EOW brane, which consequently induces a finite tension.}
elements.sent-2411-410: As a result, the brane moves to a position with constant angle.
elements.sent-2411-411: The corresponding bulk action in this setup is given by \begin{equation}\label{DES-action} I=I_0+\int_Q\,\sqrt{h}L_Q\end{equation} where $\mathcal{L _\mathcal{Q}$ corresponds to the Lagrangian of a CFT$_2$ with large central charge; typical examples may be cooked up in terms a large number of massless scalar fields or in terms of the Liouville action \cite{Chen:2020uac .}}
elements.sent-2411-412: The Neumann boundary condition \eqref{NBC is modified through the expectation value of the stress tensor of this conformal matter theory as \cite{Takayanagi:2011zk,Deng:2020ent,Chu:2021gdb \begin{equation}\label{Modify-NBC} K_{ab}=(K-T)h_{ab}+ 8\pi G_N \left<T_{ab}\right>~~,~~\left<T_{ab}\right>=\frac{2}{\sqrt{h}}\frac{\partial L_Q}{\partial h_{ab}}\end{equation} and the EOW brane is essentially treated as a defect in the bulk geometry\footnote{Note that since the EOW brane has an AdS$_2$ profile, which is maximally symmetric, the stress tensor expectation value in the defect CFT$_2$ is proportional to induced metric $h_{ab}$ and only affects the tension on the EOW brane.}}}
elements.sent-2411-413: In this sense, the CFT$_2$ degrees of freedom on the EOW brane do not really act as dynamical fields backreacting on the ambient geometry, and the EOW brane may be treated as a lower dimensional defect.. .
elements.sent-2411-414: In the modified bulk scenario with defect conformal matter on the EOW brane, the entanglement entropy of an interval in the original BCFT includes contributions from the defect matter.

elements.paragraph-2411-004: On inclusion of matter in the bulk :
elements.sent-2411-417: It is important to emphasize that incorporating conformal matter localized on the EOW brane does not require the introduction of additional sources in the holographic dictionary \cite{Deng:2020ent .}
elements.sent-2411-418: Rather, the conformal matter acts as a modification to the boundary conditions placed at the conformal boundary $x=0$ \cite{Deng:2023pjs .}
elements.sent-2411-419: The conformal matter is placed on the EOW brane in order to obtain a lower dimensional effective theory more suited to the island paradigm, where one requires the same quantum field theory to reside on the entire hybrid manifold.
elements.sent-2411-420: Furthermore, as the inclusion of such matter theory does not backreact on the ambient geometry, it is safely assumed that cut-off prescription for the holographic dual of $T \bar{T}$-deformed CFTs is still applicable in the large $N$ approximation \cite{Deng:2023pjs .}

elements.sent-2411-465: In this article, we investigate the structure of mixed state entanglement and correlation through the reflected entropy in an AdS$_3$ braneworld model equipped with a finite distance Dirichlet wall, putatively dual to some boundary conformal field theory with $T\bar{T}$ deformation in the spirit of \cite{McGough:2016lol .}
elements.sent-2411-466: In particular, we focus on an AdS$_3$ geometry characterized by a finite radial cut-off, induced by the $T\bar{T}$ deformation, which is truncated by an end-of-the-world (EOW) brane containing defect matter.
elements.sent-2411-467: As discussed in \cite{Deng:2023pjs , such a model can naturally arise from a partial reduction, and the resulting braneworld gravity is governed by Neumann boundary conditions on the EOW brane, while we apply AdS/CFT duality for Dirichlet boundary conditions on the finite cut-off surface.}
elements.sent-2411-468: In this context, we extend the defect extremal surface (DES) prescription, essentially a double-holographic counterpart to the island formula, for computing the reflected entropy of bipartite mixed states within the defect AdS/BCFT correspondence.
elements.sent-2411-469: The DES formula offers a novel approach to understanding entanglement in such deformed setups, bridging insights from both holographic duality and boundary theories.
elements.sent-2411-470: We then extend our analysis to time dependent configurations involving an eternal black hole coupled to a radiation bath in the effective lower-dimensional setup.
elements.sent-2411-471: In this context, we compute the reflected entropy between subsystems in the black hole interior, between black hole and radiation subsystems, and between subsystems in the radiation bath utilizing both the island and DES prescriptions.
elements.sent-2411-472: We also examine the time evolution of reflected entropy for these configurations and plot the analogous of the Page curves for the reflected entropy, considering scenarios with and without the $T\bar{T}$ deformation.
elements.sent-2411-473: Once again, we find that the results from boundary and bulk calculations are consistent up to the leading order in the radial cut-off for time-dependent cases, providing a strong consistency check for the proposal discussed earlier.
elements.sent-2411-474: It is important to note that, as shown in \cite{He:2019vzf,Chen:2018eqk,Jeong:2019ylz the effects of the $T\bar{T}$ deformation in the boundary theory is captured by modifying the correlation functions by appropriate insertions of stress-tensor components within the framework of conformal perturbation theory.}
elements.sent-2411-475: However, in this article, we take a different route and impose that the effects of deformation are encapsulated in the induced metric on the radial cut-off surface, similar to \cite{Deng:2023pjs .}
elements.sent-2411-476: It will be interesting to explicitly verify the credibility of this approach by developing a perturbation theory for the $T\bar{T}$-deformed BCFT from scratch.
elements.sent-2411-477: Although our doubly holographic computations conform to the results from the island formula in the leading order, a critical issue remains to be addressed.
elements.sent-2411-478: Namely, the well-known breakdown of the holographic proposal in \cite{McGough:2016lol upon the inclusions of bulk conformal matter.}
elements.sent-2411-479: A possible way out is to consider the alternative proposal of mixed boundary conditions \cite{Guica:2019nzm which can address the presence of matter in the bulk.}
elements.sent-2411-480: We leave a critical re-examination of the present work in the context of modified asymptotic boundary conditions for the future.
elements.sent-2411-481: A potential higher-dimensional extension of the present work, as well as that of \cite{Deng:2023pjs , possibly in the spirit of \cite{Hartman:2018tkw , warrants further investigation due to the subtleties associated with incorporating conformal matter on the defect brane.}}
elements.sent-2411-482: Moreover, even in the undeformed case, the DES and island prescriptions do not align consistently in higher dimensions, and the process of partial dimensional reduction necessitates the inclusion of all Kaluza–Klein modes \cite{Chu:2021gdb .}
elements.sent-2411-483: Resolving these challenges is crucial for developing a robust higher-dimensional framework that accurately captures the effects of $T\bar{T}$ deformations and the dynamics of conformal matter on defect branes.
elements.sent-2411-484: There are several other future directions to explore.
